implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Executes an HTTP GET request to the JSONPlaceholder users endpoint, filters users by city, converts the filtered response to CSV format, and writes the CSV file to persistent storage.
implementation.name: main
implementation.pseudo_code:
CALL fetch(USERS_URL)
IF response not OK THEN THROW error
PARSE response JSON into users array
CALL filterUsersByLocation(users)
DEFINE CSV headers for selected fields
FOR EACH user IN filteredUsers:
    CONVERT user fields to CSV row using userToRow
JOIN headers and rows into full CSV string
ENSURE data directory exists
WRITE CSV string to data/users.csv
PRINT success message

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Maps a user object to an array of CSV-safe strings for each predefined column, used by main() to generate the CSV rows.
implementation.name: userToRow
implementation.pseudo_code:
RETURN array[
    id,
    escaped name,
    escaped username,
    escaped email,
    escaped address.street,
    escaped address.city,
    escaped address.zipcode,
    escaped phone,
    escaped website
]

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Escapes special characters to ensure CSV-safe output.
implementation.name: escapeCsvField
implementation.pseudo_code:
CONVERT input to string
IF string contains comma, quote, or newline THEN
    RETURN string wrapped in quotes with internal quotes doubled
ELSE
    RETURN string

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Reads the saved CSV file, converts it to JSON, and returns the result over HTTP.
implementation.name: GET /api/users
implementation.pseudo_code:
IF users.csv does not exist THEN
    RETURN 404 with error JSON
READ users.csv
PARSE CSV into JSON array using csvToJson
RETURN JSON array

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Converts the generated CSV back into a JSON array for the API endpoint.
implementation.name: csvToJson
implementation.pseudo_code:
SPLIT CSV into non-empty lines
IF less than 2 lines THEN RETURN empty array
PARSE header line into header list
FOR EACH row line:
    PARSE row into values
    MAP values to header keys
RETURN array of row objects

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Parses a single CSV line with support for quoted fields so that CSV rows can be converted back into JSON.
implementation.name: parseCsvLine
implementation.pseudo_code:
INITIALIZE result array, current string, inQuotes flag
FOR each character in line:
    IF quote THEN
        TOGGLE inQuotes or append escaped quote
    ELSE IF comma AND NOT inQuotes THEN
        PUSH current to result
        RESET current
    ELSE
        APPEND character to current
PUSH final field
RETURN result

implementation.How_is_it_connected_to_implementation: Filters the fetched users to only include those whose address.city equals 'Boone' before CSV transformation.
implementation.name: filterUsersByLocation
implementation.pseudo_code:
INPUT users array
RETURN users WHERE user.address.city EQUALS 'Boone'

missing_implementations: CSV file contains columns corresponding to each field in the user JSON object (the implementation only selects a subset of fields).
CSV column names are identical to the field names in the JSON object (current implementation uses custom header definitions).
No user data is omitted or transformed; all fields from the API response are included (current implementation omits multiple fields such as company, geo coordinates, suite, etc.).
If the API request fails or the response is empty, the system does not create or modify the CSV file and signals an error. Empty-response handling is missing; an empty CSV is still written.
